name: Collada de Lois Trail (PR-LE 18)
name_original: Collada de Lois desde Reyero – trail running circular por el Parque Regional Montaña de Riaño y Mampodre (PRC-LE 18)
description: This difficult 5.5-mile (8.8 km) jogging trail in Parque Regional Montaña de Riaño y Mampodre offers great views of Lois and its "Catedral d
region_intro: Jogging around Reyero offers access to the natural landscapes of the Montaña de Riaño y Mampodre Regional Park in León, Spain. The region is characterized by varied terrain, including dense forests and open meadows, with views of the Picos de Europa. These natural features provide diverse visual interest for runners. The area's trails are suitable for exploring its rich flora and fauna. Best jogging routes around Reyero The most popular jogging route is La Cervatina – running circular from Puebla de Lillo (PR-LE 28), a 9.3 miles (15.0 km) trail that takes 1 hour 45 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers a circular path through the natural surroundings. Another top favourite among local runners is Collada de Lois Trail (PR-LE 18), a difficult 5.5 miles (8.8 km) path. This trail is known for its natural beauty and varied terrain. Local runners also love the Susarón – El Torreón loop from Puebla de Lillo, a 5.6 miles (9.0 km) trail leading through mountainous landscapes, often completed in about 1 hour 45 minutes. Jogging around Reyero is defined by mountainous terrain, dense forests, and open meadows. The network offers options for different ability levels, with 1 moderate and 12 difficult jogging routes available. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 100 runners have used komoot to explore Reyero's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Reyero?
A: There are 13 dedicated running routes around Reyero listed on komoot. The majority, 12 of them, are classified as difficult, with one moderate option available for those seeking a less challenging run.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the running trails in Reyero?
A: The running trails in Reyero offer a diverse and picturesque landscape. You can expect mountainous terrain, dense forests, and open meadows. Many routes provide breathtaking views of the Picos de Europa, making for a visually engaging run through the Montaña de Riaño y Mampodre Regional Park.

Q: Are there any easy or moderate running options in Reyero for beginners?
A: While most of the running routes in Reyero are classified as difficult, there is one moderate option available. This allows beginners or those looking for a less strenuous run to still enjoy the beautiful natural surroundings. The region's varied terrain ensures there's something for different fitness levels.

Q: Which running routes offer the best scenic views in Reyero?
A: Many routes in Reyero provide stunning vistas. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20159695" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Pico Susarón loop from Puebla de Lillo&lt;/a&gt; offers impressive mountain scenery. The region as a whole is known for its views of the Picos de Europa and its rich flora and fauna, which can be observed along many trails.

Q: Are there any long-distance running trails in Reyero?
A: Yes, for runners looking for longer distances, routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1055942014/la-cervatina-running-circular-from-puebla-de-lillo-pr-le-28" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Cervatina – running circular from Puebla de Lillo (PR-LE 28)&lt;/a&gt; cover approximately 9.3 miles (15 km). The region's extensive network of trails, including those adapted from hiking and mountain biking, provides opportunities for longer runs.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Reyero?
A: The gentle spring and autumn temperatures make these seasons particularly ideal for outdoor activities like running in Reyero. During these times, the landscape bursts with wildflowers and lush greenery, enhancing the natural beauty of your run. Summers can be warm, and winters may present colder conditions.

Q: Are there any circular running routes available in Reyero?
A: Yes, several running routes in Reyero are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A popular example is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1055942014/la-cervatina-running-circular-from-puebla-de-lillo-pr-le-28" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Cervatina – running circular from Puebla de Lillo (PR-LE 28)&lt;/a&gt;. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24568328" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Susarón – El Torreón loop from Puebla de Lillo&lt;/a&gt;, which also offers a circular path.

Q: What kind of natural features or landmarks can I see while running in Reyero?
A: While running in Reyero, you can enjoy diverse natural features such as dense forests, open meadows, and views of the Picos de Europa. Notable landmarks and points of interest in the vicinity include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/306845" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gilbo Peak&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4214355" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Yordas (1,967 m)&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5553221" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Esla Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, offering stunning panoramic views.

Q: Are there any specific trails known for their natural beauty?
A: The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1056015337/collada-de-lois-trail-pr-le-18" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collada de Lois Trail (PR-LE 18)&lt;/a&gt; is particularly known for its natural beauty and varied terrain, making it a favorite among local runners. The entire region, being part of the Montaña de Riaño y Mampodre Regional Park, offers rich flora and fauna to observe.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Reyero?
A: Runners using komoot often praise the natural landscapes of Reyero, highlighting the varied terrain, from dense forests to open meadows, and the stunning views of the Picos de Europa. The opportunity to explore the rich flora and fauna while running is also a frequently mentioned positive aspect.

Q: Are there any challenging running routes for experienced runners?
A: Yes, Reyero is well-suited for experienced runners seeking a challenge, with 12 out of 13 routes classified as difficult. Trails like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24567654" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Peña Armada&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20159695" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Pico Susarón loop from Puebla de Lillo&lt;/a&gt; offer significant elevation gains and demanding terrain.
tips: Near a large block, we stumbled upon this nice refuge. On our way to the Lois pass, after the refuge, there is a spring on the right, off the track.
From Collada de Lois, we have magnificent views of the valley that descends to the town of Lois and the mountains that surround us.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to jog the Collada de Lois Trail (PR-LE 18)?
faqs.answer: The komoot data suggests an average jogging duration of approximately 1 hour and 16 minutes for this 8.8 km circular route. However, given its 'difficult' rating and significant elevation gain, experienced trail runners might complete it faster, while others may take longer, especially on challenging sections.

faqs.question: Is the Collada de Lois Trail suitable for beginner joggers?
faqs.answer: This trail is considered a challenging jog, especially for beginners. It features a significant elevation gain of around 377 meters over 8.8 km, with sections through an oak forest, an unforested ridge, and a limestone crest. There's also a 'strong descent' that requires careful foot placement. While generally stable, casual joggers might find parts too steep or uneven and may prefer to walk those sections.

faqs.question: Where does the Collada de Lois Trail (PR-LE 18) start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The trail starts from Reyero, initially following a road before entering the trail behind the church. You can find amenities and likely parking at the Reyero Recreational Area, which serves as a convenient starting point for the circular route.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Collada de Lois Trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied and engaging. You'll ascend through a robust oak forest, then an unforested ridge offering panoramic views. The route continues along a limestone crest known as Los Cabezos before reaching Collado Reyero and Collada de Lois. The descent from Recubiles includes a 'strong descent' section. The trail is noted to be 100% cyclable, indicating a generally firm and wide path, though the steep and rocky sections require attention.

faqs.question: What are the best times of year to visit the Collada de Lois Trail?
faqs.answer: To fully enjoy the trail and its views, it's best to visit outside of the snowy season. Spring, summer, and autumn generally offer more favorable conditions for jogging and hiking, allowing for safer passage and better visibility of the diverse landscapes.

faqs.question: What notable sights and viewpoints can I expect along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers magnificent panoramic views, especially from the open ridge and from Collada de Lois, where you'll see the secluded Río Dueñas Valley and the charming village of Lois with its famous 'Catedral de la Montaña'. You'll also pass by a shelter in the Montaña de Riaño y Mampodre. During rainy seasons, numerous torrents form waterfalls along the slopes of the Valderriero Valley.

faqs.question: Is the Collada de Lois Trail (PR-LE 18) dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the trail is within a natural park, specific regulations regarding dogs are not detailed in the available information. Generally, dogs are welcome on trails in natural areas, but it's always advisable to keep them on a leash, especially given the presence of wildlife like black kites and European honey buzzards, and to respect other trail users.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for the Collada de Lois Trail or the Parque Regional Montaña de Riaño y Mampodre?
faqs.answer: The Collada de Lois Trail (PR-LE 18) is located within the Parque Regional Montaña de Riaño y Mampodre. Typically, regional parks in Spain do not require entrance fees for general access to trails. However, it's always good practice to check the official park website for any specific regulations, especially concerning activities like camping or large group events, though no permits are generally needed for hiking or jogging this specific route.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife and flora might I encounter on the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail traverses a thriving oak forest with patches of sessile oak and large common oaks. Keep an eye out for local wildlife; black kites and European honey buzzards have been observed in the area. The diverse landscape supports a rich ecosystem.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities like cafes or accommodation near the trailhead in Reyero or Lois?
faqs.answer: The starting point at the Reyero Recreational Area may offer some basic amenities. For more options, the nearby village of Lois, famous for its 'Catedral de la Montaña', is likely to have cafes or small guesthouses, providing a place to refuel or stay after your activity.

faqs.question: What is the recommended direction for jogging the Collada de Lois Trail?
faqs.answer: The recommended direction is to ascend via the Valdehigüende pass. This involves gaining height through the oak forest and then an unforested ridge, allowing you to enjoy panoramic views on the way up. The descent from Recubiles, while strong, brings you back towards the Arroyo de Valderriero and completes the circular route.
